Serving All of Milam County, TX

Milam County Stucco Services

Milam County is home to some of the most character-rich older homes in Central Texas. From the historic neighborhoods of Cameron to the quiet streets of Rockdale, stucco exteriors here tell a story. Our team specializes in restoring and repairing stucco on homes that have stood for decades, bringing walls back to life while preserving the charm that makes these properties special.

Get a Free Stucco Estimate Call Now: (512) 706-9699

Stucco Restoration for Milam County Homes

Many homes across Milam County were built during periods when stucco was the exterior finish of choice. Over the years, the combination of Texas summer heat, seasonal storms, and natural settling has left its mark on these walls. Cracks, discoloration, and water intrusion are common issues that homeowners in Cameron, Rockdale, and the smaller communities in between deal with regularly.

Our approach to stucco work in Milam County starts with understanding the age and construction of your home. Older stucco systems often require different repair techniques than newer installations. We take the time to assess what is going on beneath the surface before recommending any work, so the repairs we make are long-lasting.

Whether you own a turn-of-the-century home in downtown Cameron or a mid-century ranch near Milano, we bring the right methods and materials to match your existing finish and protect your home for years to come.

Stucco Services We Offer in Milam County

Professional stucco and plaster solutions for homes and businesses throughout Milam County.

Stucco Repair

Aging stucco in Milam County often shows hairline cracks, chipping, or areas where the finish has pulled away from the substrate. We carefully remove damaged material, prep the surface, and apply repair coats that blend with your existing walls so the finished result looks seamless.

Learn More

Stucco Installation

For homeowners ready to update an older exterior or add stucco to a new addition, we handle full installations from lath and scratch coat through the final color finish. Every installation is built for the local climate and designed to last.

Learn More

Stucco Crack Repair

Foundation movement and thermal expansion are the leading causes of stucco cracks in older Milam County homes. We evaluate each crack to determine the underlying cause, then apply the appropriate repair method so the fix holds up over time.

Learn More

Stucco Water Damage Repair

Water damage behind stucco walls can go unnoticed for years, slowly deteriorating sheathing and framing. We identify moisture intrusion points, remove compromised stucco, address any structural concerns, and restore the exterior to a watertight condition.

Learn More

Exterior Plaster

Several historic homes in Cameron and Rockdale feature traditional lime-based exterior plaster. We are experienced with heritage plaster systems and can match the original texture and composition when making repairs or restoring full wall sections.

Learn More

EIFS Repair & Installation

EIFS systems on commercial buildings and newer homes in Milam County require specialized care. We repair delamination, seal moisture entry points, and install new EIFS assemblies that meet current building standards while improving energy performance.

Learn More

Commercial Stucco

From storefronts along Highway 77 to agricultural buildings throughout the county, we handle commercial stucco projects of all sizes. Our team works around business hours and project timelines to minimize disruption to your operations.

Learn More

Why Milam County Homeowners Trust Us

Deep experience with older stucco systems found on homes built before 1970

Color and texture matching so repairs blend with original finishes

Thorough moisture assessments before any work begins

Honest evaluations that distinguish cosmetic issues from structural concerns

Locally scheduled crews who understand rural project logistics

Written warranties on all restoration and repair work

Local Expertise

We understand Milam County soil types, weather patterns, and building codes for every community.

Fully Licensed & Insured

Licensed Texas contractor with full liability and workers' compensation coverage for every Milam County project.

Fast Response

Prompt estimates and scheduling for all Milam County communities. Most projects begin within 1–2 weeks.

Common Stucco Problems We Fix in Milam County

Older homes in Milam County face stucco challenges that are directly tied to the age of the structure and decades of Texas weather. Here are the most common issues we see across Cameron, Rockdale, and surrounding areas.

Cracks

Foundation settling is common on Milam County properties built on the region's expansive clay soils. As the ground shifts, stucco walls develop cracks that range from fine surface lines to deeper structural fractures.

Bubbling or soft stucco

When moisture gets trapped behind stucco on older homes that lack modern moisture barriers, the finish can bubble or become soft to the touch. This often indicates decay of the underlying lath or sheathing.

Water stains

Heavy rains and aging rooflines on historic Milam County homes often direct water onto stucco walls. Over time, this leaves dark stains and mineral deposits that are difficult to remove without proper treatment.

Window and door leaks

Original window and door frames on older homes frequently lose their seal against the stucco wall, creating gaps where rainwater enters the wall cavity and causes hidden damage.

Failing patches

Past repair attempts using mismatched materials or improper technique are a recurring issue on Milam County properties. These patches often crack, discolor, or separate from the surrounding stucco within a few years.

Separation around trim

Wood trim on older homes expands and contracts with moisture changes, gradually pulling away from the stucco finish and leaving open joints that invite water and insects.

Impact damage

Hail, fallen branches from mature pecan and oak trees, and everyday wear can chip or gouge stucco surfaces. Leaving these spots unrepaired exposes the substrate to moisture.

Faded or stained exterior walls

Decades of direct sun exposure cause pigmented stucco finishes to fade unevenly. Algae and mildew growth on shaded north-facing walls adds to the worn appearance of many older homes in the area.

How We Work in Milam County

A clear, straightforward process from first call to finished project.

1

Schedule Inspection

Contact us to set up a convenient time for an on-site evaluation. We visit properties throughout Milam County, from Cameron to Thorndale and everywhere in between.

2

Review Scope

During the inspection, we examine your stucco for cracks, moisture damage, and structural issues. We document our findings and discuss what needs to be addressed right away versus what can wait.

3

Get Estimate

You receive a clear, written estimate that breaks down the cost of each repair or restoration phase. There are no hidden fees and no pressure to commit on the spot.

4

Complete Work

Once approved, our crew completes the work on the agreed timeline. We handle cleanup, walk you through the finished result, and provide care instructions for your restored stucco.
